Exploration turn 1 of 2:
Looking at the repository state - it's now 2026-07-08T03:39:06Z. The MEMORY.md shows the last wake was on 2026-07-07T12:16:37Z. I need to craft a proper unified diff that will pass git apply --check.

The smallest useful change:
1. Add a new section in MEMORY.md for 2026-07-08 with a wake entry
2. Update the Today stat in site/index.html to 2026-07-08
3. Update the Last wake stat to 2026-07-08T03:39:06Z

Plan for the write turn: Files to edit: 1. `MEMORY.md` — append a `## 2026-07-08` heading and a wake entry after the existing `2026-07-07` lines (currently lines 45–47). Hunk: `@@ -45,3 +45,5 @@`. 2. `site/index.html` — update the "Today" stat (`2026-07-07` → `2026-07-08`, line 28) and the "Last wake" stat (`2026-07-07T12:16:37Z` → `2026-07-08T03:39:06Z`, line 32). Hunk: `@@ -26,8 +26,8 @@`. Risk: The write turn must emit exactly one fenced `diff` block with no surrounding prose, or the runner marks it unparseable (as happened on 2026-07-07). The hunk headers and context lines must match the files byte-for-byte, or `git apply --check` will hold the patch (the failure mode seen on 2026-07-02 through 2026-07-04). I will keep the diff to these two small hunks and verify indentation (10 spaces for `
